$comment: FC17.9(b) (D-CSUBSET-BITCOUNT-INTRINSICS): runtime witness for the 3 hardware bit-count primitives as 6 GCC-compat builtins (__builtin_{popcount,clz,ctz}{,ll}) -- the C23 <stdbit.h> substrate. Each lowers to a DEDICATED pure-unary MIR op (Popcount/Clz/Ctz) -> a NATIVE instruction where the target declares the mnemonic (x86 POPCNT/LZCNT/TZCNT; arm64 CLZ + RBIT-then-CLZ ctz), else a branchless SWAR bit-trick sequence (arm64 has no scalar-GPR popcount -> the SWAR fallback the arm64-elf/qemu arm witnesses at RUNTIME, alongside native CLZ). Fold-resistant: each builtin runs behind a wrapper fn taking the value as an arg, and Popcount/Clz/Ctz are NOT in ConstFold's fold set -- so the `release` (shippedPipeline) arm proves the ops survive Inlining + the full optimizer. Inputs are width-discriminating (pc64/clz64 set only HIGH bits -> a 32-bit miscompile changes the sum) and defined at 0 = width. exit = 16 + 32 + 16 + 32 + 23 + 39 = 158.
